name: structured-list
description: Tabular row list for scannable data, optionally single-selectable

meta.name: StructuredList
meta.description: Tabular row list for scannable data, optionally single-selectable

props.name: items
props.type: { id: string; cells: ReactNode[]; selected?: boolean }[]

props.name: selectable
props.description: When true, rows can be selected.

examples.title: Selectable
examples.code: <StructuredList selectable defaultValue="a" items={[{ id: "a", cells: ["Ada"] }, { id: "b", cells: ["Grace"] }]} onSelect={(id) => set(id)} />

whenToUse: Presenting a set of related records in aligned columns the user scans top to bottom
Letting the user pick exactly one row from a short, readable list (selectable)
Carbon-style structured content where a full data table is heavier than needed
whenNotToUse: Sorting, filtering, pagination, or multi-select over many rows — use DataTable
Free-form vertical content without column alignment — use List

antiPatterns.bad: selectable with multiple rows marked selected
antiPatterns.good: A single selected id (value/defaultValue) — selection is single-choice
antiPatterns.why: The selectable variant is a radiogroup; multiple selected rows break the radio semantics

related.name: DataTable
related.reason: DataTable adds sorting, filtering, and pagination; StructuredList stays static and lightweight

related.name: List
related.reason: List is single-column vertical content; StructuredList aligns multiple cells per row

intent.a11yRationale: Static lists use table/row/cell roles so columns are announced; selectable lists become a radiogroup of role=radio rows with roving tabindex, so arrow keys move focus and Enter/Space check a row exactly like native radios

flexibility.note: Toggle selectable based on whether the list is interactive or purely presentational
Static uses table semantics; selectable uses radiogroup — do not mix
